Infineon Sensors Distributor - Hall, Pressure, Current, Radar Full Series

Infineon Technologies delivers one of the broadest sensor portfolios in the semiconductor industry, covering magnetic sensing, pressure measurement, current sensing, and millimeter-wave radar in a single product family optimized for both automotive and industrial end markets. The TLE Hall-effect sensor lineup spans latching switches, speed sensors, and angle sensors, all manufactured on a proprietary magnetic BiCMOS process that achieves sub-1 mT sensitivity while rejecting common-mode stray fields from adjacent current-carrying conductors. The DPS pressure sensor series represents Infineon's contribution to high-accuracy MEMS sensing for consumer, industrial, and medical applications. The DPS310 combines a resistive pressure sensing element with a MEMS temperature sensor on a single die, achieving +/-0.06 hPa relative accuracy at 1 Hz output data rate - sufficient for floor-level altitude determination in indoor navigation and ventilation control systems. For current sensing in power conversion and motor drive applications, the TLI current sensor family uses a coreless transformer approach that eliminates the magnetic saturation issues of traditional Hall-based current sensors, achieving bandwidths above 150 kHz with intrinsic galvanic isolation. The XENSIV(TM) radar sensors - KIT_XMC4700_RADAR and BGT60TR13C - bring 60 GHz FMCW radar to industrial presence detection and gesture recognition in form factors smaller than a postage stamp. BeiLuo distributes Infineon sensors with particular depth in the Hall-effect and pressure segments, maintaining stock of over thirty active part numbers across the TLE4900, TLE4966, and DPS families. Our FAE engineers bring hands-on calibration experience and can provide Python and embedded C reference code for accelerating sensor integration in customer platforms.

FAE Technical Support

Sensor selection questions are where BeiLuo's FAE engagement genuinely adds value. I regularly help customers choose between the TLI4966G dual-channel Hall speed sensor and the TLE5012B angle sensor depending on whether they need rotational direction detection or absolute angle output. For pressure applications, the DPS310's SPI and I2C interfaces with hardware FIFO make it far easier to integrate than discrete sensing bridges with external ADCs. BeiLuo maintains evaluation boards for the most commonly requested sensor families and our team can provide complete driver code to cut typical sensor bring-up from days to hours.

Get FAE Consultation

Need more Sensors options or bulk pricing? Contact our FAE team.
